title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are infusions made from various plants and spices that can help freshen breath and improve oral hygiene. These teas often contain antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Examples of herbal teas that are known to help with bad breath include peppermint, spearmint, sage, cinnamon, and clove. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals can experience fresher breath, improved digestion, and a reduction in the bacteria that cause bad breath. This can lead to increased confidence, better social interactions, and overall improved quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties. The menthol in peppermint oil has been shown to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath, while also freshening and cleansing the mouth. Additionally, the cooling sensation from the tea can help stimulate saliva production, which washes away bacteria and food particles, leaving the breath feeling clean and fresh.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth that cause bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol, reduce inflammation in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath, and also soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat.
Stimulant properties: Peppermint tea's stimulants, such as menthol and camphor, increase saliva production, which helps wash away bacteria, food particles, and other debris that can cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other oils that help freshen breath by reducing plaque and gingivitis.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that have antibacterial properties, combating bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Heat water in a saucepan or kettle to a boil and let it cool for five minutes.
Add the measured peppermint leaves to the cooled water and allow it to steep for ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Pour the tea into a cup and serve hot, garnishing with fresh mint leaves if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with bad breath because it possesses natural antibacterial properties that can combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth. These bacteria are often responsible for causing bad breath, also known as halitosis. The tea's antiseptic qualities help to reduce the amount of bacteria in the mouth, thereby eliminating bad breath. Additionally, lemon balm teas' ability to soothe and calm the digestive system can also contribute to fresher breath by reducing inflammation and discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm tea's antibacterial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth and throat that can cause bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea, like citral, reduce inflammation in the gums, tongue, and throat, which can contribute to bad breath and other oral health issues.
Antifungal properties: Lemon balm tea's antifungal compounds, such as limonene, help combat fungal infections in the mouth, including Candida overgrowth, which can cause halitosis (bad breath).
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and germs that cause bad breath, promoting a fresher mouth.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that inhibit the growth of oral pathogens, reducing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lemon balm leaves measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Measure two cups of filtered cold water into a teapot and add one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves.
Heat the water to a boil then reduce heat to simmer for five to seven minutes to infuse the tea.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional two to three minutes before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria responsible for halitosis. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans and other microorganisms that contribute to bad breath. Drinking herbal thyme tea regularly can help eliminate these bacteria from the mouth, reducing volatile sulfur compounds that cause unpleasant odors.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath by reducing their ability to multiply and produce volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs).

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by decreasing the production of enzymes that break down food particles and toxins.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants, such as flavonoids, may help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antibacterial properties that help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath, reducing its occurrence and severity.
Bark: The bark of the thyme plant contains tannins, which provide astringent properties that can help dry up excess saliva and reduce inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers.
Heat 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot for 3-5 minutes.
Combine the heated water with the thyme leaves and flowers in a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to blend.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with bad breath because it has natural antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria in the mouth, responsible for causing bad breath. The antioxidants present in sage also reduce inflammation in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, sage's astringent properties help dry up excess saliva, which can trap bacteria and cause odor-causing compounds to form. By drinking sage tea regularly, you can help freshen your breath and maintain good oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage tea's antibacterial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth and throat, which can cause bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help to reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth tissues that can contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Sage tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used to make tea due to their ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause bad breath.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant contains antioxidants and astringent properties, making it effective in reducing oral infections and neutralizing volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sage leaves according to your preference weighing about one teaspoon per serving.
Heat water in a pot or kettle to a rolling boil and then reduce heat to simmer for five minutes.
Add the harvested sage leaves to a teapot or infuser measuring about one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes allowing the flavors and aromas to fully integrate and blend.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve hot or allow it to cool to your desired temperature before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antimicrobial properties that combat the bacteria responsible for causing halitosis. The essential oil present in oregano, carvacrol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans and other oral bacteria that contribute to bad breath. By reducing the levels of these bacteria, oregano teas help eliminate volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for the unpleasant odors associated with bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Oregano tea's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus and Candida albicans, by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from multiplying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in oregano tea, like carvacrol and thymol, reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can help alleviate symptoms of gum disease, a common cause of bad breath.

Antioxidant properties: Oregano tea's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the mouth from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of oregano are used in teas because they have antibacterial properties that help eliminate bacteria causing bad breath.
Leaves: The leaves of oregano are a popular choice for teas as they contain antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that freshen the breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried oregano leaves from origanum vulgare plant and measure out one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil then remove from heat.
Add the measured dried oregano leaves to the boiling water and allow it to steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids.
Serve the herbal oregano tea hot, garnished with lemon slice or honey if desired, after preparation time of approximately seven minutes total.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which effectively eliminate odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The herb's essential oils also have a freshening effect on the breath, leaving a clean and minty sensation. Additionally, rosemary's antiseptic qualities help to reduce inflammation in the gums and tongue, further reducing bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals in the mouth and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which is often a root cause of bad breath.

Antibacterial properties: Rosemary tea contains antibacterial compounds that can help to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to bad breath, such as Helicobacter pylori and Streptococcus mutans.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacteria that cause bad breath.
Leaves: Known for their deodorizing and antiseptic properties, which help to neutralize and eliminate bad breath-causing volatile sulfur compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves and rinse them with cool water to remove impurities.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves or two teaspoons of fresh rosemary leaves per cup.
Combine the measured rosemary with boiling hot water in a teapot or tea infuser for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor intensity.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids, then sweeten with honey if desired before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties. The essential oils present in cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath. Additionally, the warm and soothing properties of the tea help to relax the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment, which in turn helps to eliminate bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Cinnamon's antimicrobial properties help to combat the bacteria that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans, by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from multiplying.
Antioxidant properties: Cinnamon's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by damaging oral tissues and promoting bacterial growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums and mucous membranes of the mouth, which can cause bad breath and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cinnamon roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth, relieving symptoms of bad breath.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the cinnamon plant has antibacterial properties that help eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried cinnamomum verum bark per cup of boiling water.
Steep the cinnamon bark for five minutes in a tea infuser or strainer.
Add honey and lemon to taste as desired for sweetness and flavor balance.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth if needed.
Serve hot immediately after preparation or let it cool for later consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate the underlying causes of bad breath. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and menthol, work together to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, specifically in the tongue, gums, and cheeks. This reduction in bacterial growth leads to a significant decrease in volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs), which are responsible for bad breath. As a result, herbal Tasmanian blue gum teas provide a natural and effective way to freshen breath and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea, such as eucalyptol and α-pinene, help to combat the bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and reducing their ability to produce volatile sulfur compounds.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing redness and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea, including vitamin C and polyphenols, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by promoting healthy cell growth and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant are used to create a tea that helps combat bad breath by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria in the mouth.
Bark: The bark of the plant is rich in compounds that have antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Rinse 20-30 grams of fresh eucalyptus leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the rinsed eucalyptus leaves into small pieces and combine them with 1 quart (960 ml) of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and let it cool before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint teas helps with bad breath because its natural oils, such as menthol and carvyl acetate, have antibacterial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth. These beneficial compounds also help to reduce plaque and tartar buildup on teeth, preventing the release of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that cause halitosis. As a result, drinking spearmint tea can leave your mouth feeling clean and fresh, eliminating bad breath and promoting good oral health.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Spearmint tea's high menthol content helps to freshen breath by killing bacteria that cause bad breath and reducing volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that contribute to halitosis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of spearmint tea help to inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, thereby preventing the production of VSCs that can cause bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Spearmint tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the gums, tongue, and other parts of the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath by creating an environment conducive to bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, alleviating bad breath.
Leaves: The leaves contain menthol, which has natural antibacterial properties that can help kill bacteria causing bad breath, as well as refreshing the breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried spearmint leaves measuring one tablespoon per eight ounces of water.
Rinse the harvested spearmint leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed spearmint leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, allowing the flavors and oils to meld together.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh strainer or tea infuser to remove the spearmint leaves.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, including terpenoids and flavonoids, work to eliminate the bacteria that cause halitosis (bad breath) by reducing their growth and preventing them from producing volatile sulfur compounds. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth, further combating bad breath and promoting a fresh, clean feeling.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including those that cause gum disease and other oral infections, which contribute to bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can alleviate symptoms of bad breath such as a dry mouth and swollen gums.

Antioxidant properties: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the teeth and oral tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bad breath and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the narrow-leaved paperbark plant are rich in antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath.
Bark: The bark of the narrow-leaved paperbark plant contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it effective in reducing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in amounts of one teaspoon for every eight ounces water.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool running water and gently pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine the cleaned leaves with boiling water in a teapot or tea infuser, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding any solids and retaining the flavorful liquid.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool to room temperature for consumption, taking care to store any leftover tea in an airtight container.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bad breath with herbal teas is by choosing teas with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, such as chamomile, sage, and peppermint. These teas inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath. Moreover, they promote saliva production, which helps to wash away odor-causing particles. Regularly drinking these herbal teas after meals and before bed can significantly improve breath fresheness. However, it's essential to maintain good oral hygiene, including brushing, flossing, and tongue scraping, for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal teas include digestive issues like bloating, gas, and constipation. Herbal teas such as chamomile, peppermint, and ginger are known for their soothing properties and can help alleviate symptoms of these ailments. Chamomile tea is often used to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, while peppermint tea can aid in digestion and relieve gas and bloating. Ginger tea is well-known for its ability to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, some herbal teas like licorice root and marshmallow root can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing. However, it's important to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for any ailment.
